feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Man with same name as US Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible for Alaska's primary ballot, judge rules
entry.content: An Alaska judge has ruled that a man with the same name and party affiliation as Alaska Republican U.S. Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible to challenge the senator in the August primary

entry.title: States seek to lower drug prices by targeting the companies that manage them for health plans
entry.content: Legislators across the U.S. are trying to lower prescription costs by reining in big companies that handle drug coverage for health insurers

entry.title: OpenAI launches a limited preview of GPT-5.6 for a 'small group of trusted partners'
entry.content: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 comes in three variants, including its most powerful and its most affordable models yet.

entry.title: Polygamous sect leader convicted of abuse charges after girls found in trailer
entry.content: A polygamous sect leader has been convicted on child abuse charges in Arizona while already serving a 50-year sentence in federal prison for orchestrating sex involving children

entry.title: Newly released video shows Connecticut prison officers striking inmate before he died
entry.content: A Connecticut judge has publicly released a video showing correctional officers striking an inmate multiple times and pepper spraying him before he died in 2018

entry.title: World Cup debutants Cape Verde continues fairy-tale run to knockouts
entry.content: The fairy-tale for Cape Verde continues as the tournament debutants proceeds to the knockout stage.

entry.title: Anthropic gets US government's permission to redeploy its Mythos cybersecurity AI model
entry.content: Anthropic is restoring select organizations' access to Mythos after getting permission from the US government.

entry.title: Complaint seeks answers on Scouting America's transgender policy after Pentagon deal
entry.content: A gay rights activist is suing the Defense Department to answer a simple question: Did Scouting America actually ban transgender members in a deal with the Pentagon

entry.title: Democrats Are in a Civil War—and the Far Left Is Gaining Ground
entry.content: Centrists who have been “wringing our hands” are now scrambling to beat back insurgents.

entry.title: SpaceX will join Nasdaq-100
entry.content: Adding SpaceX this quickly would make the Elon Musk company one of the first beneficiaries of Nasdaq's recently adopted fast-track inclusion framework.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Respect yourself and others will respect you.
Lucky Numbers: 85, 58, 40, 58, 1, 65

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Want to remember something in the future? Send a text to yourself and schedule it to be delivered on the appropriate date.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Because metal was scarce, the Oscars given out during World War II were made of wood.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Franklin D. Roosevelt
entry.content: “Do Something. If it works, do more of it. If it doesn't, do something else.” — Franklin D. Roosevelt

entry.title: On 10th anniversary, Boleros de Noche's legacy is celebrated by L.A. City Hall
entry.content: In honor of the 10th anniversary of concert series Boleros de Noche, L.A. declares Aug.1 'Día del Bolero.'

entry.title: A Minnesota man accused in a $250M fraud scheme has been taken into custody in Somalia
entry.content: Authorities say a Minnesota man charged with orchestrating a $250 million fraud scheme has been taken into custody in Somalia

entry.title: Trump orders retaliatory airstrikes against Iran
entry.content: President Trump ordered retaliatory airstrikes against Iran for violating the ceasefire agreement by attacking a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

entry.title: Miraculous rescues after deadly Venezuela earthquakes
entry.content: There have been incredible rescues in Venezuela two days after those back-to-back earthquakes. NBC News’ George Solis reports.

entry.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
entry.content: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

entry.title: Tom Sandoval's girlfriend Victoria Robinson arrested after patio fire pit altercation
entry.content: Court documents and video reveal a fight involving a lit fire pit that led to the domestic battery arrest of Tom Sandoval's girlfriend Victoria Robinson.

entry.title: Exclusive-China's Geely to ship first Lotus EVs to Canada in July under Carney-Xi deal, ambassador says
entry.content: Exclusive-China's Geely to ship first Lotus EVs to Canada in July under Carney-Xi deal, ambassador says

entry.title: Trump's Great American State Fair opens with melted ice cream, empty booths
entry.content: Trump's Great American State Fair opens with melted ice cream, empty booths

entry.title: Gracie the Giraffe, who went missing after wandering off a Texas ranch, is finally found
entry.content: Gracie the Giraffe has finally been found in Texas

entry.title: Election worker says federal officers confronted her at polls over social media post criticizing ICE
entry.content: A poll worker says two federal officers visited her at a voting location during New York’s party primaries to confront her about a social media post she’d written criticizing the ICE officer who fatally shot Renee Good in Minneapolis

entry.title: A mesmerizingly vulnerable Angelina Jolie fails to fully redeem 'Couture'
entry.content: Set during a busy Paris Fashion Week, the movie threads together the stories of three working women whose interior lives only sometimes register.

entry.title: White House mandates move away from overdose prevention in US health programs
entry.content: Experts say move signals greater political interference into public health and will exacerbate opioid overdose crisisHealth programs receiving federal funding must agree within days to new priorities from the Trump administration, including a focus on “parental authority” in education and a move away from proven overdose-prevention methods like harm reduction, suggesting greater political control over public health.The new priorities will likely affect progress against the opioid crisis, and they could signal an attack on vaccination requirements at schools, which are set at the state and local level. The priorities may also weaponize public health to quash “public disorder.” Continue reading...

entry.title: Supreme Court says Alito’s verbal reaction to Sotomayor was based on a ‘misunderstanding’
entry.content: Supreme Court says Alito’s verbal reaction to Sotomayor was based on a ‘misunderstanding’

entry.title: Buttigieg was briefly separated from his children after police say he was target of false report
entry.content: Buttigieg was briefly separated from his children after police say he was target of false report

entry.title: It's a dumb time to buy an Xbox, even with the coming price hike
entry.content: This is a prime example of a bad deal.

entry.title: Texas board approves adding Bible stories to required reading for public school students
entry.content: The reading list will take effect starting in 2030.

entry.title: Minnesota fraud suspect arrested in Somalia after 4 years on the run, officials say
entry.content: Abdikerm Eidleh, accused of playing a key role in the Feeding Our Future fraud scheme, was arrested in Somalia after more than four years, federal officials said.

entry.title: Judge orders DOJ to either unredact more Epstein files or explain why it can't
entry.content: A judge on Thursday ordered the Justice Department to either release unredacted versions of several files on the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein or explain why it can't do so.

entry.title: Federal Judge Declares Mistrial in Palisades Arson Case
entry.content: A California jury failed to reach a consensus on whether Jonathan Rinderknecht deliberately sparked the deadly fire that destroyed thousands of Los Angeles homes.

entry.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 28)
entry.content: Jane Pauley hosts a special program celebrating our nation's semiquincentennial, "These United States - America at 250."

entry.title: Trump says Iran violated ceasefire with drone strike on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: President Trump on Friday accused Iran of violating the ceasefire with the U.S. after Iranian drones attacked commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

entry.title: Judge holds Charlie Kirk murder case prosecutors in contempt for comments about guilt
entry.content: The Utah judge in the murder case over conservative activist's Charlie Kirk’s killing has held prosecutors in contempt of court over comments they made about defendant Tyler Robinson's guilt

entry.title: Convicted rapist who faked death and fled U.S. has died
entry.content: Nicholas Rossi​, 38, was serving at least 10 years in prison in Utah following his convictions in 2025 in two sexual assault cases.

entry.title: Fear grips Haitian communities after Supreme Court ruling unwinds protection from deportation
entry.content: Fear is ricocheting through Haitian communities across the United States after the Supreme Court decided to allow the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants fleeing violence and natural disasters

entry.title: Former Indiana governor explains how his nonprofit will help workers impacted by AI boom
entry.content: RAISE US, a nonprofit group, launched a new fund to help ease the transition for workers impacted by AI. Former Indiana Gov. Eric Holcomb, a co-chair of RAISE US, joins to discuss.

entry.title: Peter Asher has heard it all
entry.content: The veteran producer and manager is the subject of a new documentary. At 82, he's still got work to do.

entry.title: Vote for WSJ’s House of the Month
entry.content: Our picks for June include a California home inspired by George Washington, a Colonial in Maine or a Colorado estate built around old mining sheds.

entry.title: CFTC is conducting an investigation into Polymarket, source says
entry.content: The probe comes after the Commodity Futures Trading Commission and the Department of Justice dropped previous inquiries into Polymarket last July.

entry.title: DC reaches settlement with man who protested troops' patrol with Darth Vader song
entry.content: The District of Columbia has reached a settlement agreement for an undisclosed amount of money with a resident who claims police illegally detained him for following an Ohio National Guard patrol while playing Darth Vader’s theme song from “Star Wars” ...

entry.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
entry.content: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

entry.title: Is your phone charger wasting electricity when it's not charging?
entry.content: Homes with many phone chargers pay more even when they're not using them.

entry.title: Black women's non-profit awards grants to local Southern groups as part of joy-filled tour
entry.content: Organizations supporting Black women and girls in the American South are receiving a boost this summer

entry.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
entry.content: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

entry.title: How a Star Wars Lego Dispute Triggered an Armed Police Raid in Utah
entry.content: It started with a consignment gone bad. It ended with officers storming an Airbnb, a restraining order, a RICO lawsuit—and a small town turned upside down.

entry.title: Trump Says Iranian Attack on Cargo Ship Was Violation of Ceasefire Deal
entry.content: The attack on Thursday has snarled shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and threatens the main goal of the president’s preliminary deal to wind down the war.

entry.title: MS NOW weekend anchor Alex Witt to exit as network reduces live weekend programming
entry.content: Witt will be succeeded by Antonia Hylton, as 'The Weekend: Primetime' is canceled.

entry.title: They're finally too old for it in the middling clip reel 'Jackass: Best and Last'
entry.content: After four gross-out features that occasionally reached cinematic greatness, Johnny Knoxville and the gang gather for a hasty and underwhelming final hurrah.

entry.title: Sigh. Again? 'Love Island USA' confirms another contestant fired over apparent use of racial slur
entry.content: 'Love Island USA' contestant Alannah Keyser, facing backlash for apparently using a racial slur in a recently resurfaced social media post, has left the villa for good.

entry.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
entry.content: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending. OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]

entry.title: 'Unfortunately, no': Shreyas Iyer confirms Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will have to wait for India debut
entry.content: Young batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will have to wait for his historic India debut. Captain Shreyas Iyer confirmed the 15-year-old was not in the playing XI for the first T20I against Ireland, citing team balance. Despite the delay, Sooryavanshi, who had a stellar IPL and India A season, remains on the cusp of becoming India's youngest international cricketer.

entry.title: The Opening Finals 2026: Live Updates from Day 2
entry.content: The Opening Finals is officially back following a six-year hiatus. The Nike Football event gets underway on Thursday from Beaverton, Oregon and will run through Friday. More than 100 prospects across the 2027, 2028 and 2029 recruiting cycles are in attendance this week at the Philip H. Knight Campus. Prospects earned invites to the finals […]

entry.title: Atletico Madrid star tells Barcelona target to chase his dream – ‘It’s a personal matter’
entry.content: The Julian Alvarez-to-Barcelona saga has been all over the news in the past couple of days after his public declaration asking to leave Atletico Madrid this summer.Alex Baena has offered his view on t...

entry.title: Four-time Super Bowl champion OT leads Steelers 2026 countdown to kickoff: Day No. 79
entry.content: The Pittsburgh Steelers are 79 days away from their 2026 NFL season opener, and here’s a look at which players wore No. 79 best.

entry.title: Australia eye 'something special' after reaching World Cup last 32
entry.content: Coach Tony Popovic said Australia want to do "something special" after reaching the World Cup knockout rounds for only the third time in their history."I'd like to think that we dominated the game in a crucial World Cup qualifier with a very young squad in the third match when everything's on the line.

entry.title: Duckett & Stokes haul England back into third Test
entry.content: Ben Duckett’s scintillating century builds on an inspirational spell from Ben Stokes to haul England back into the decisive third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge.

entry.title: AFC Wimbledon appoint Morrison as First Team coach
entry.content: Michael Morrison is appointed as AFC Wimbledon first-team coach after retiring from playing at the end of last season.

entry.title: Mercedes' Antonelli tops Friday practice in Austria
entry.content: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli heads McLaren's Oscar Piastri in Friday practice at the Austrian Grand Prix.

entry.title: Trump threatens 100% tax on European imports
entry.content: President Donald Trump is threatening a 100% tax on imports from any country that imposes a tax on digital services from United States companies

entry.title: Aircraft Crashes Into Beijing’s Tallest Building
entry.content: The rush-hour incident in a tightly controlled airspace came weeks after Beijing prohibited the flying of drones in the Chinese capital.

entry.title: "Regime Change" authors on Trump's "information bubble" and use of power
entry.content: In "Regime Change, Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump," Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan describe his fundamentally different approach to running the country.

entry.title: Senate Democrats push for info on "anti-weaponization" fund, demand hearing
entry.content: Led by Sen. Cory Booker of New Jersey, a group of Senate Democrats suggested the Justice Department violated its internal policies with the creation of the "anti-weaponization" fund.

entry.title: Utah governor restricts fireworks as largest US wildfire surges uncontained
entry.content: Utah's governor has restricted fireworks as the largest wildfire in the nation continues to grow

entry.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining classified information
entry.content: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.

entry.title: College Football 27 QB rankings led by Dante Moore, Julian Sayin
entry.content: EA Sports College Football 27 top 10 quarterback rankings include Arch Manning, John Mateer, Trinidad Chambliss, CJ Carr, Darian Mensah. See ratings.

entry.title: Striker Okoronkwo signs two-year deal at Doncaster
entry.content: Doncaster Rovers sign striker Frankie Okoronkwo on a two-year deal following his release by Everton this summer.

entry.title: Bristol City sign Dutch midfielder Yah
entry.content: Bristol City sign midfielder Gibson Yah from Dutch club Volendam on a four-year deal for an undisclosed fee.

entry.title: U.S. Center for SafeSport sees increase in reports, quicker resolution time in abuse cases
entry.content: Benita Fitzgerald Mosley took over as SafeSport CEO in February.

entry.title: Newcastle have to signal some form of ambition again
entry.content: The transfer window has not long been open, but some of the Premier League's highest-earning clubs have their eyes on Newcastle's best players. Tottenham have already made an unsuccessful opening offer for Sandro Tonali, while Arsenal hold an interest in the Italian as well as fellow midfielder Bruno Guimaraes. Tonali is a key player at Newcastle, but it is hard to overstate Guimaraes' importance - the Brazilian really is this team's heartbeat.

entry.title: 🚨Official: Dylan Gorosito signs new Boca Juniors deal
entry.content: The Argentine player extended his contract with Xeneize until December 2030. A product of the youth academy and with strong performances whenever he was called up to the senior team, Dylan Gorosito ca...

entry.title: Zach Ertz shares emotional reflection of his nine seasons with Eagles
entry.content: Years after leaving the Eagles, Zach Ertz still gets emotional when discussing the teammates and city who helped define his career.

entry.title: Former Purdue basketball player Carson Barrett joins App State
entry.content: After spending the past 10 years playing and coaching at Central Catholic and Purdue, Carson Barrett is leaving home for a Division I coaching gig.

entry.title: Jalen Brunson's mom on teaching him to be a leader: "It's how you respond"
entry.content: The Second Round Foundation, founded by Sandra and Jalen Brunson, aims to help provide young people with what they need to thrive in the future.

entry.title: Go behind the scenes with Druski ahead of the BET Awards
entry.content: Comedian and entertainer Druski will host the BET Awards on Sunday. At 31, he's the youngest host in the show's history. He gave "CBS Mornings" co-host Nate Burleson a preview of what to expect.

entry.title: Newsom proposes federal billionaire tax and AI "public equity" fund
entry.content: As California Gov. Gavin Newsom eyes a 2028 presidential bid, he's calling for a national tax on billionaires and a public stake in AI, though he opposes a state ballot measure to tax billionaires.

entry.title: Sandra Brunson on helping son Jalen Brunson lead: "It's not what happens, it's how you respond"
entry.content: Sandra Brunson, the mother of Knicks star Jalen Brunson, talks with "CBS Mornings" about how she helped her son become a leader and the nonprofit they formed together to help young people.

entry.title: Slow progress toward peace deal as Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: Hezbollah says Israel must withdraw forces from Lebanon and Iran strikes a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as negotiations toward a peace deal grind on.

entry.title: Lawmakers Call for Federal Probe of Polymarket Over Deceptive Advertising
entry.content: Two senators asked regulators to probe Polymarket’s promotion of fake bets following the Journal’s reporting.

entry.title: Florida aims to impeach judge who freed killer mom using COVID insanity defense
entry.content: Florida's attorney general is seeking to impeach a judge who let a mother walk free after she claimed COVID-19-induced psychosis caused her to drown her baby and stab her family.

entry.title: Leon Black Tells Congress He Didn’t Pay Epstein for Women
entry.content: The co-founder of Apollo said he was duped by the man he hired as a financial adviser, according to remarks prepared for Congress.

entry.title: Jeremy Grantham says this is 'the most expensive market in 'American history'
entry.content: Jeremy Grantham says soaring valuations around AI makes the U.S. stock market historically expensive.

entry.title: Love Island USA removes second contestant for using racial slur
entry.content: Love Island USA removes second contestant for using racial slur

entry.title: Warsh reaches within the Fed for latest advisory appointments
entry.content: Warsh has added two more key advisors as he seeks to remake how the central bank approaches the economy and monetary policy.

entry.title: Leon Black Tells Congress He Didn’t Pay Epstein for Women
entry.content: The co-founder of Apollo said he was duped by the man he hired as a financial adviser, according to remarks prepared for Congress.

entry.title: Iranian national U.S. sought for $3.4 billion in hacking attacks arrested
entry.content: An Iranian national who is wanted by the U.S. for mass hacking attacks that caused $3.4 billion in damage was arrested in Montenegro, police there say.

entry.title: Walker Buehler never imagined returning to Dodger Stadium like this
entry.content: Walker Buehler returns to Chavez Ravine in the most unlikely jersey.

entry.title: Timberwolves' Anthony Edwards has no more excuses after LaMelo Ball blockbuster
entry.content: Anthony Edwards facing extra pressure with Timberwolves' LaMelo Ball trade.

entry.title: Billionaire Leon Black expected to testify Friday before House Epstein panel
entry.content: An investor who employed and was close to Jeffrey Epstein is scheduled to appear before members of Congress investigating the deceased sexual abuser.

entry.title: Where is Didier Deschamps? Explaining France coach's absence from World Cup game vs. Norway
entry.content: France will be without their coach for their final World Cup group game against Norway.

entry.title: Race to find survivors after Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes
entry.content: The back-to-back earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 230 people, and the toll is likely to keep rising as rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

entry.title: How to watch World Cup 2026: TV channels, live streams and fixture schedule for FIFA tournament
entry.content: The FIFA World Cup is considered the most illustrious competition on the global football calendar.

entry.title: The Long-Term Threat to the Memory Chip Boom Is Innovation
entry.content: Micron makes clear the shortage will last, but memory customers are developing workarounds.

entry.title: Mark Tame obituary
entry.content: My friend Mark Tame, who has died of lung cancer aged 61, spent his life caring for others. He worked for various charities (including Mind) in Bristol and London that advocated for mental health patients, and spent the past five years at Homerton healthcare NHS foundation trust in London as a care coordinator after the Covid-19 pandemic.Mark was born in Wivenhoe, Essex, the son of Anne Tame, a telephonist, and John Matthews, a telephone engineer, who met in the postwar period at Bletchley Park, the codebreaking centre in Buckinghamshire. He went to several different schools, then Bristol University, where he studied social politics, which became an anchor of not only his career, but of his personal ethics. Continue reading...

entry.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Opens Lower as Tech Selloff Deepens
entry.content: Chip makers lead declines; oil edges closer to pre-war levels

entry.title: King Charles will not live in Buckingham Palace after $487 million renovation
entry.content: The king and Queen Camilla have decided instead to continue living at nearby Clarence House, a smaller, stately home.

entry.title: Wimbledon 2026 live draw: Updated bracket, seedings for Serena Williams and more in singles, doubles
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live results from Friday's draw, including where Williams will start her Wimbledon journey in singles and doubles.

entry.title: What channel is the NHL Draft on today? Start time, TV schedule, live stream, picks order for 2026 coverage
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the 2026 NHL Draft, including TV channel and streaming options for each round.
